122919.explanation: The LVR stands for appointing high-quality aldermen regardless of gender, origin, or orientation.

122920.explanation: The residents of our city elect the municipal council once every four years. Residents can express their opinions through citizen participation. Referendums will lead to more bureaucracy and slower decision-making. Moreover, referenda are very expensive.

122923.explanation: With a fixed closing time after which cafés and restaurants can decide for themselves how late they stay open within that time frame.

122925.explanation: The LVR is always positive about new developments but prioritizes local and regional companies over national and multinational chains.

122926.explanation: It is not up to us to close asylum centers; we have a legal quota to comply with. The Hague must decide whether asylum centers close or remain open.

122928.explanation: The use of the stove guide must be encouraged.

122930.explanation: It was national policy to impose a fireworks ban; we will not spend local community money on it. It costs hundreds of thousands of euros per year to hold a fireworks show.

122931.explanation: We believe that bulky waste should be collected periodically at home, so it can be properly recycled, and we believe this will also reduce illegal dumping in public spaces.

122933.explanation: We believe this is not the responsibility of a municipality but of the national government to provide resources. We do ensure that both public buildings and public spaces are accessible; that is the municipality's responsibility.

122935.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives funds (legally mandated) from the municipality. No additional funding is needed for basic tasks. If a (joint) plan for additional qualitative local broadcasting arises, we can discuss possible extra funding.

122940.explanation: To improve safety in Roermond, we want more camera surveillance in places where it is needed.

122942.explanation: BOAs (Public Order Officers) must be able to defend themselves if they find themselves in a dangerous situation. The baton may only be used when necessary.

122948.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage; quick/temporary solutions must be implemented in the upcoming period, supported where necessary with one-time funds to address this.

122949.explanation: We believe everyone must contribute. People on social assistance should also do meaningful social work (as far as they are able). This also fosters involvement.

122951.explanation: In Roermond, this has already been raised to 130% of the social assistance level.

122953.explanation: Many new charging points have been added in recent years, and a plan to install charging points has been established. We think this is sufficient for now; only if it turns out there are too few charging points should more be considered, which is not currently the case from our perspective.

122955.explanation: We do not want to introduce paid parking in any more neighborhoods.

122956.explanation: We believe that all of Roermond deserves clean air; we do not want to differentiate between the city center and other parts of Roermond. Simply banning cars in the center is symbolic politics.

122961.explanation: Prices should cover the costs of the purpose and therefore should not be more expensive than necessary.

122962.explanation: At the right locations, this should be possible to create more jobs.

122964.explanation: We believe our own residents, who have often been waiting and searching for a home for years, should be given priority.

122966.explanation: We are against sacrificing green spaces or farmland for solar panel installations; primarily roofs should be used, which are still underutilized.

122968.explanation: There should be sufficient cultural centers spread throughout Roermond.

122971.explanation: The LVR ensured that the number of coffeeshops was reduced from two to one. We want to keep it at one - no more, no less.

122972.explanation: Roermond already has many social rental homes. For new construction, we want 30% social rental and also a percentage of mid-range rent to encourage more movement in the housing market. Otherwise, people remain stuck in social housing.

122975.explanation: There should be sufficient centers spread throughout Roermond.

122976.explanation: The rates are high enough and do not need to increase. We believe the municipality should not profit from parking fees; they should not unnecessarily rise.

122977.explanation: Residents and visitors to the center must be able to park properly.

122979.explanation: A careful process is already underway, and the LVR believes that if the outlet expands, Roermond should also benefit. It is important to first complete the ongoing process carefully.

122980.explanation: A drop-in center must be established in a suitable location after careful consideration.

122919.explanation: The executive board should reflect society, which consists of half women and half men.

122920.explanation: It is a form of citizen participation, and a referendum can be part of that.

122923.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports night permits and staggered closing times.

122925.explanation: There are sufficient distribution centers nearby. Roermond focuses on sustainable jobs.

122926.explanation: Everyone fleeing from an unsafe situation has the right to be well cared for. Roermond takes its responsibility in this.

122928.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes the nuisance and health complaints caused by wood burning.

122930.explanation: The municipality must be able to offer an alternative due to the fireworks ban.

122931.explanation: Bulky waste should be collected free of charge twice a year for every household, so this is possible even for people with limited means. This will help prevent illegal dumping.

122933.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports a society in which everyone can participate. Under conditions, we are in favor; for example, making historic buildings accessible.

122935.explanation: It is good that there is a qualitatively good local broadcaster at the local level. Therefore, it is worthwhile to consider whether project-based support is needed.

122940.explanation: Camera surveillance is a heavy measure that must be used carefully and sparingly as a last resort to protect privacy.

122942.explanation: Currently, there are no indications in Roermond that this is necessary. Our BOAs do not need to perform police tasks. Therefore, a baton is not necessary.

122948.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes this applies to all young people.

122949.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA encourages people on social assistance to make a meaningful contribution, but we are against coercion.

122951.explanation: We are currently at 130%, and we want to increase to 150% of the social minimum.

122953.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA promotes the possibilities of electric driving; we also look at options for houses without a garden or driveway.

122955.explanation: Parking space should primarily be available for residents, and the cost of the permit should be kept as low as possible.

122956.explanation: It is outdated to enter the city center with polluting vehicles.

122961.explanation: The municipality of Roermond incurs high costs to facilitate our visitors, so we want to increase the tourist tax.

122962.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA primarily looks at vacancy on the current business parks (infill before expansion). Expansion is allowed under the condition of high-quality jobs and production. Never at the expense of nature.

122964.explanation: Everyone who flees is entitled to safe shelter. The pressure on the housing market is so great that we reserve part of the social rental homes for residents with ties to Roermond. Housing status holders is a legal obligation, which we fulfill.

122966.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports solar panels on roofs and parking spaces. Agricultural land can be used for housing, nature, and food security.

122968.explanation: We have several cultural centers in Roermond. Every neighborhood should have a community facility where local cultural associations can meet.

122971.explanation: We currently have a good location for the coffee shop.

122972.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes there should be a good distribution of social and affordable housing, so that an affordable home can be found in your own neighborhood.

122975.explanation: Every neighborhood should have a community facility where local associations can meet.

122976.explanation: Paid parking for visitors to the city center may become more expensive. This will reduce parking pressure for residents. We support free parking for the disabled.

122977.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA focuses on good public transport to and from the city center, ensuring that street parking spaces are available for our residents. Visitors can use the parking garage.

122979.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds this negotiable if the Designer Outlet produces sustainably, invests in good employment, and a tax on the Outlet is introduced.

122980.explanation: In any case, within walking distance of the station.

122919.explanation: It is up to the parties in the coalition to form the executive board. We welcome diversity. At the same time, attention to quality is essential.

122920.explanation: We are not in favor of a referendum. However, we advocate promoting citizens' initiatives, participation, and involvement in other ways.

122923.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the freedom within certain limits to determine their opening hours. We advocate quiet periods at night and, if possible, on Sundays. The current approach reflects this.

122925.explanation: The economy is an important driver to increase our social impact on the community. Every initiative deserves to be properly evaluated. This requires careful consideration with regard to livability.

122926.explanation: Every municipality has a responsibility and role in the reception of migrants. This solidarity must not be lost. The implementation of this reception must be thoughtful and involve local residents.

122928.explanation: We are not in favor of too many rules and regulations. However, it is important that the municipality makes residents aware of the harmful effects that (can) result from using wood stoves.

122930.explanation: The CDA strongly supports a fireworks ban. A well-organized fireworks show can contribute to this.

122931.explanation: Residents have the option to dispose of their bulky waste free of charge at the recycling center.

122933.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve space. It is their own responsibility and interest to organize this. However, the municipality contributes to making the city, village, and neighborhood accessible for people with disabilities and encourages this initiative among entrepreneurs through cooperation.

122935.explanation: A local broadcaster deserves financial support. The national government provides funds for this. The CDA advocates ensuring a diverse and accessible program in consultation with the local broadcaster. Modern media channels (online) can be utilized for this.

122940.explanation: In principle, this should not be necessary. But people still experience certain outdoor areas as unsafe. In such environments, camera surveillance can contribute to a safer living environment.

122942.explanation: Expanding the equipment of enforcement officers requires careful consideration. If circumstances require it, this is negotiable. For now, the situation in Roermond does not warrant adding a baton to the equipment.

122948.explanation: At present, this problem does not really occur in Roermond. It is not a student city. To boost the housing market, the municipality could consider (temporary) homes for starters.

122949.explanation: It entirely depends on the personal situation. Tailored to the possibilities, the municipality can encourage people on welfare to contribute.

122951.explanation: Roermond already opts for a higher income limit. The CDA agrees with this.

122953.explanation: The growing number of electric cars demands this.

122955.explanation: As long as parking pressure in residential areas is manageable, this is unnecessary.

122956.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of introducing environmental zones in a small provincial city.

122961.explanation: Raising taxes should never be the starting point. The challenges a municipality faces should be central.

122962.explanation: Roermond has the largest business park in Limburg and several smaller ones. Not only expansion, but also improvement and infill of existing parks should be central.

122964.explanation: It always requires careful consideration to give priority status to residents of any origin.

122966.explanation: Land is scarce. There is little agricultural land in Roermond. Let's preserve it as much as possible for its primary purpose. Banning goes too far. A proper balance is required.

122968.explanation: Maasniel has community buildings and facilities. If these become unsuitable, new construction can be considered. For now, the need does not seem to exist.

122971.explanation: The CDA is basically not in favor of offering and using (soft and hard) drugs. Roermond has a coffee shop that meets current demand.

122972.explanation: Roermond is around the national average. Building should meet needs and demand. Surrounding municipalities also have tasks in this area. For now, using a 30% target is the CDA's starting point.

122975.explanation: The CDA strongly supports sufficient facilities close to residents. If that calls for local neighborhood centers, we are happy to advocate for that.

122976.explanation: More expensive is not a goal in itself. Parking costs should contribute to a good distribution of parking pressure throughout the city. This requires a careful parking policy.

122977.explanation: Space is limited. There are several parking garages. If parking pressure in existing facilities becomes too high, it is advisable to consider expanding parking options on the edges of the city center.

122979.explanation: Do not exclude these kinds of matters outright. It is important to make a careful assessment. For the CDA, we cooperate with expansion if it contributes to the future sustainability of the entire city. Otherwise, do not (or no longer) proceed!

122919.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen values diversity but does not choose a mandatory distribution. Quality and suitability of aldermen take priority.

122920.explanation: On important issues, residents must be able to directly express their voice. A referendum is one option but is not always the right tool. Demokraten Swalmen finds resident participation very important.

122923.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to do their work. Within clear rules for livability of the surroundings and safety, there should be room for customization. Entrepreneurs deserve space to operate well in Roermond.

122925.explanation: Economic developments must fit our neighborhoods, villages, and Roermond. Economic growth is not a goal in itself, especially if it causes more traffic and disturbance in the landscape.

122926.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen chooses a human and above all realistic approach. The idea of 'as quickly as possible' does not fit with responsibility and care.

122928.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen acknowledges that there are health and environmental effects but opts for good information instead of a general ban.

122930.explanation: In case of a national fireworks ban starting 2026, a central fireworks show in Roermond can ensure more safety and less nuisance, provided it is well organized.

122931.explanation: Collecting bulky waste prevents people from dumping their waste carelessly and makes our neighborhoods and centers more livable.

122933.explanation: Accessibility is a responsibility for everyone, but primarily the task of the entrepreneur themselves. The municipality can support and advise entrepreneurs.

122935.explanation: Local journalism is important for democracy and for residents’ engagement.

122940.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance in certain places can help with persistent nuisance if used carefully.

122942.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen weighs carefully; safety is important, but arming Enforcement Officers must be carefully considered.

122948.explanation: Temporary solutions can help reduce pressure on the housing market if they are necessary and fit within the living environment.

122949.explanation: If possible, participation is very important for residents receiving social assistance. It helps with rhythm, structure, and social contacts. The municipality encourages this where possible and provides good guidance.

122951.explanation: The municipality of Roermond already does a lot to support residents with a smaller income. Where possible and necessary, we examine if and how this support can be expanded. At the same time, we want to encourage residents to return to (more) work if possible.

122953.explanation: Charging points for electric cars are important but must be located appropriately. It is important that parking pressure in the area does not become too high as a result.

122955.explanation: Introducing parking permits is not a goal in itself. It is only done if this is the best solution for the parking situation in a neighborhood. We also do not want to unnecessarily burden residents with extra costs.

122956.explanation: We find it important that the municipality of Roermond promotes clean transportation, but we do not want to unnecessarily hinder entrepreneurs.

122961.explanation: We want to revise the tourism tax so that visitors who stay briefly pay a fair share of municipal costs. Visitors who stay longer are taxed relatively less because they contribute more to the local economy.

122962.explanation: Employment is important for Roermond, but expansion must be carried out carefully. This must be at the right place and under the right conditions.

122964.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen believes everyone has the right to an appropriate home, whether someone is a status holder or a resident of Roermond. We look at the personal situation of the home seeker, not at someone’s background.

122966.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen opts for customization: use roofs and paved areas first before utilizing agricultural land or nature reserves.

122968.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen believes every neighborhood, center, or village deserves a good living room. If there is demand for a new cultural center in Maasniel, we will explore whether this is feasible.

122971.explanation: The current coffeeshop is in a better location than in the city center and causes little nuisance. For the future, it will be examined whether it is feasible and desirable to close the coffeeshop in Roermond.

122972.explanation: We build the right homes in the right places, including many social homes. They fit the needs of each neighborhood and center, for starters and seniors, as well as for small and large families.

122975.explanation: Every neighborhood, center, and village deserves a suitable neighborhood facility with a living room function. There, people can meet, and social connection is encouraged.

122976.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen believes visitors in the center of Roermond should park as much as possible in municipal parking garages.

122977.explanation: Demokraten Swalmen believes visitors in the center of Roermond should park as much as possible in municipal parking garages.

122979.explanation: Designer Outlet Roermond must never replace the city center. Expansion: no, unless the basics are first in order and livability, traffic, parking, sustainability, and broad prosperity have been thoroughly investigated. The interests of our residents are paramount.

122980.explanation: Roermond must have good drop-in facilities for homeless people. The location must fit; it does not necessarily have to be near the station.

122919.explanation: SPR stands for diversity without compromising quality.

122920.explanation: SPR stands for active citizen participation in the broadest sense of the word.

122923.explanation: Assuming that the rules in the Hospitality Act are respected. The municipality should not be patronizing.

122925.explanation: New warehouses, new style in existing locations.

122926.explanation: Asylum reception in our municipality deserves our support.

122930.explanation: Joint fireworks shows foster togetherness and contribute to a safe New Year's Eve.

122931.explanation: Collection at home for a small fee.

122933.explanation: Entrepreneurs must engage in socially responsible business, and there are sufficient requirements for this under national legislation.

122935.explanation: A fee of €1 per resident is paid in accordance with the Media Act.

122942.explanation: This is important for the safety of BOA officers and citizens, and we will continue to advocate for an additional body camera.

122949.explanation: Customization must remain possible, but there are many positive aspects to allowing people who rely on this temporary solution to participate in society. It provides meaning, routine, and satisfaction and gives something back to society.

122951.explanation: With the current inflation, this is more than necessary.

122953.explanation: In certain neighborhoods there is insufficient charging capacity. Now that Enexis is upgrading the power grid, the Municipality should respond accordingly.

122955.explanation: General visitors, clients, and residents should jointly seek a solution: space is limited.

122956.explanation: On busy days the city center is overburdened. Environmental badges are successfully applied in other countries.

122961.explanation: Roermond receives many tourists, and they should contribute to the infrastructure and development of the city.

122962.explanation: There is still sufficient space around Roermond to establish industrial estates. We believe in renewing existing sites.

122964.explanation: There are national rules for status holders and asylum seekers. The problem lies in the housing shortage.

122966.explanation: We have just started installing solar panels on roofs.

122968.explanation: It’s about building a cultural center in the right location.

122971.explanation: The current solution works and is sufficient.

122972.explanation: Roermond is currently only building unaffordable homes, thus blocking a large group of residents from being able to live in the Municipality.

122975.explanation: Citizen participation and community spirit are the DNA of a neighborhood and the city.

122976.explanation: Entrepreneurs in the city depend on customers who already pay enough in the Municipal parking garages. Prices should be equalized and parking in garages encouraged.

122977.explanation: There is sufficient capacity in the city center; there is no retail that needs parking ‘in front of the door’. Public transport by bus and train should be encouraged.

122979.explanation: Growth is certainly allowed but requires adjustments for traffic and congestion. The DOC will need to operate more socially and connect with the city.

122980.explanation: This implies that there is only a problem in the station area; a walk-in center must be located strategically in the right place.

122919.explanation: The VVD considers it important that women are politically represented. Therefore, almost half of the top 10 on our list consists of highly qualified women. However, each party is responsible for nominating candidate aldermen themselves. We have no influence over this.

122920.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of referenda. Issues are often not captured by a simple 'yes' or 'no'. We do find it important to actively involve residents prior to decision-making, but the decision is ultimately taken by the elected municipal council.

122923.explanation: The VVD wants everyone to have a pleasant hospitality experience: visitors, operators, and residents. Therefore, within agreed frameworks, we offer as much freedom as possible regarding opening hours. This allows operators to respond to the tight labor market while respecting residents’ quality of life.

122925.explanation: Online orders require the presence of distribution centers. The VVD does not rule out new distribution centers in advance. However, space is limited: we prefer to prioritize large-scale construction of new residential areas or expansions of existing local businesses.

122926.explanation: The VVD hopes the asylum reception in Roermond can close as soon as possible. At this moment, Roermond provides temporary emergency shelter due to the legally imposed tasking and national shortage of reception places.

122928.explanation: The VVD dislikes paternalism. Many things are already prohibited, so we will not ban wood burning locally. It is important to burn good quality wood and to consider the surroundings this way. We therefore support good education about responsible burning.

122930.explanation: The VVD is supportive of a private initiative for a fireworks show in Roermond. If such an initiative arises, we want to facilitate the organizers, but organizing a fireworks show is not a core municipal task.

122931.explanation: The VVD sees value in collecting bulky waste (on request) at people’s homes. Not everyone has a van or trailer. The costs of this service are part of the waste disposal fee. We also want increased enforcement to prevent illegal dumping.

122933.explanation: As far as the VVD is concerned, everyone in our society should be able to participate. Where possible, we adjust public spaces to make buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

122935.explanation: The VVD sees a well-functioning local broadcaster as an important condition for a good local democracy. Power requires oversight, and independent journalists can provide this counterbalance. The municipality may contribute to this.

122940.explanation: The VVD is a strong supporter of camera surveillance. Cameras are an effective tool to increase chances of apprehension, document incidents, and monitor risk locations. We maximize the use of a smart camera network, always according to the rules and only where necessary.

122942.explanation: The VVD finds it important that BOA officers are equipped with the right protective equipment, such as a baton. BOAs increasingly assist the police and are often exposed to dangerous situations.

122948.explanation: The VVD would like to see more students in Roermond. This first requires the return of higher education to Roermond, but it is also conceivable that students from other cities look for housing in Roermond. We are open to this, but financing is up to the market.

122949.explanation: The VVD stands for a municipality where everyone participates according to their abilities. Social assistance is a safety net for those who cannot work, but it is also important to focus on what someone can do. A possible counter-performance is socially meaningful work.

122951.explanation: The VVD considers it important that groups on the verge of poverty receive help and support to maintain or gain control of their own lives. We see no added value in further expanding social assistance standards: there must always remain an incentive to work (as far as possible).

122953.explanation: The VVD aims to expand charging points for electric cars. The number of electric cars will continue to increase in the coming years. We must respond in time. We involve neighborhood residents to ensure charging stations are placed where they are most needed.

122955.explanation: The VVD wants residents of the center and surrounding neighborhoods to have sufficient private parking. That means we accommodate residents and their visitors with parking permits, while encouraging longer visits to the center or outlet to park in garages.

122956.explanation: The VVD supports measures that are effectively enforceable. The introduction of national zero-emission zones for city centers has been postponed. We do not find it justifiable to be stricter locally than national standards, as this would create an uneven playing field.

122961.explanation: The VVD keeps the tax burden low, also for tourists. With the city center, the Maasplassen lakes, the outlet, and surrounding nature, Roermond attracts many visitors. They stay in our hotels, eat in our restaurants, and contribute to our local economy. That is also very valuable!

122962.explanation: The VVD literally gives space to entrepreneurs to expand. Now that the industrial parks are almost fully built, we proactively look for possible new areas to accommodate business activities.

122964.explanation: The VVD opposes discrimination in the housing market, even if it concerns positive discrimination. Residents who have been waiting for a home for years get priority; no distinction is made between nationalities.

122966.explanation: The VVD applies the principle: 'solar panels on roofs'. We spare our scarce agricultural lands as much as possible. This way, we keep space to use these lands for, for example, nature recreation, agriculture, or the construction of a new residential area.

122968.explanation: The VVD is an advocate for culture in Roermond. Several locations are already available for this, such as neighborhood facilities, the ECI, Oranjerie, and The Movement. A new cultural center for Maasniel is therefore not immediately obvious.

122971.explanation: The VVD has put a lot of effort into relocating the two coffeeshops in the city center to a peripheral location on the edge of Roermond. There is no need for an additional coffeeshop. We will therefore not allow one.

122972.explanation: The VVD believes this percentage should be coordinated regionally. The directly adjacent municipalities in the Midden-Limburg region reach, at best, 20 percent social housing. A Roermond target of 35 percent would reward these municipalities for their below-average performance.

122975.explanation: The VVD advocates for a good distribution of neighborhood centers. This does not necessarily mean that every district always has its own neighborhood center, but there is always one nearby. We also look at dual use of facilities to ensure good occupancy.

122976.explanation: The VVD does not see motorists as a cash cow. Parking in the center should therefore remain affordable. Visitors who stay longer in the center are encouraged to park in parking garages. This way, street parking remains accessible for residents or for a short visit to the center.

122977.explanation: The VVD stands for sufficient parking facilities in the center. This safeguards the livability and accessibility of the city center. At the edge of the center, we need to better utilize existing space, for example by relocating the fire station to the outskirts of Roermond.

122979.explanation: The VVD wants a balance between economic interests, accessibility, and livability. Further growth without improved infrastructure, with a significant expansion of hospitality and existing agreements about branch distribution being overturned, is not in the interest of the city center and surrounding districts.

122980.explanation: Roermond already has such a facility. Currently, the station area already experiences nuisance from homeless people. An additional shelter would lead to even more visitors. As part of a well-coordinated (care) chain, we must intensify enforcement around the station.

122919.explanation: D66 wants to strive for this but it is not a goal in itself.

122920.explanation: D66 Roermond also wants to introduce a citizens' assembly.

122923.explanation: D66 wants this to be possible with respect for the environment.

122925.explanation: D66 wants to focus on innovation and sustainable employment.

122926.explanation: D66 wants to focus on small-scale shelter locations to contribute proportionally to the national shelter challenge.

122928.explanation: D66 wants to phase out the use of wood stoves and fireplaces to reduce fine dust and CO2 emissions.

122930.explanation: D66 wants to allocate the budget to other topics.

122931.explanation: D66 wants this to be done several times a year.

122933.explanation: D66 wants buildings to become more accessible for people with disabilities.

122935.explanation: D66 wants the current financial support to be maintained.

122940.explanation: D66 wants surveillance cameras at unsafe locations.

122942.explanation: D66 wants this to be addressed in cooperation with the police.

122948.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to focus on alternative housing forms such as container homes, tiny houses, or shared housing.

122949.explanation: D66 wants this to happen based on customized solutions and with the consent of the recipient.

122951.explanation: D66 wants more people to become eligible for certain schemes.

122953.explanation: D66 wants the number to grow in line with the number of electric cars.

122955.explanation: D66 does not want additional permit zones.

122956.explanation: D66 wants this to still take place with a proper transition arrangement for market traders.

122961.explanation: D66 wants this to remain the same because the municipality is financially healthy.

122962.explanation: D66 wants a healthy business climate and to focus on innovative and sustainable enterprises.

122964.explanation: D66 wants homes within the municipality to be allocated to residents and status holders based on priority and registration time.

122966.explanation: D66 wants to stimulate sustainable energy generation and use spaces multifunctionally.

122968.explanation: D66 wants the connection with Weerstand to be actively encouraged and not to compete with existing hospitality businesses.

122971.explanation: D66 wants the current policy to be continued.

122972.explanation: D66 wants this to be at least 30%.

122975.explanation: D66 wants to respond to the needs of the neighborhood. That does not mean that every neighborhood needs its own community center.

122976.explanation: D66 wants the current policy to remain as it is.

122977.explanation: D66 wants this to be linked to infill development, but there can also be homes that do not have the right to a parking space.

122979.explanation: D66 wants to organize a citizens' assembly to jointly develop a plan with the community and entrepreneurs. Currently, there is no formal plan for expansion.

122980.explanation: D66 does want a walk-in center for homeless and roofless people, but that can also be located elsewhere in the city center.

122919.explanation: Leaders must be competent and able to cooperate well. It would be ideal if the distribution is proportional, but quantity must not outweigh quality, as that harms society.

122920.explanation: For a significant issue that affects the entire society, a referendum should not be taboo. For critical matters affecting one or more neighborhoods, the municipal government can, for example, also use the Citizens' Assembly as a tool.

122923.explanation: The policy on opening hours for shops and hospitality must be determined through consultation; neither entrepreneurs nor the municipality should decide alone. The decision must serve the interests of society, not just the municipal government or a few entrepreneurs.

122925.explanation: There are already enough large warehouses built in the region, and many halls are empty. As long as they remain unoccupied for a long time, no additional square meters of hall space are needed.

122926.explanation: Asylum shelters do not need to close if everything is fine. However, distribution must be fair so that wealthier neighborhoods also take their share of the shelter facilities.

122928.explanation: Responsible use of modern stoves is already progress. Additionally, residents must realize that harmful fine particles are always released, and if neighbors experience nuisance (7% of Dutch people have lung disease), they should take this into account.

122930.explanation: One large fireworks/drone show or small ones in every neighborhood is a good alternative when private fireworks are banned. The show(s) should take place at central locations in the neighborhoods so residents can gather and celebrate New Year's together.

122931.explanation: A free, larger, and better recycling center is needed to promote reuse/recycling in the municipality. If residents have bulky waste but no acquaintances or neighbors to help, the municipality should offer a paid service to collect it.

122933.explanation: Accessibility of business premises is the entrepreneur's own responsibility. However, the municipality can help find general large-scale solutions to assist people with disabilities.

122935.explanation: The municipality must follow the guidelines of the VNG (Association of Netherlands Municipalities) and the national government and financially support the broadcaster. Additionally, extra funding is available through the Journalism Stimulus Fund.

122940.explanation: CCTV surveillance should only be installed at hotspots; the rest, according to SP, should focus on prevention instead of control. Providing more security for residents will also reduce nuisance and crime.

122942.explanation: Weapons for enforcement officers (boa's) are unnecessary when there are sufficient police officers with a short response time during conflicts/confrontations.

122948.explanation: People have the right to a home, not to live in a container. Modular building can be allowed, but it must be done with quality and prevent creating second-class citizens by housing them in containers and using them as a profit model.

122949.explanation: Community service should be requested not in exchange for benefits, but for purpose or as part of reintegration into society. Receiving welfare is not pleasant, and the municipality must do everything to help and motivate recipients to leave and stay out of welfare.

122951.explanation: Due to years of inflation and stagnant wages, it is wise to raise the income threshold. After all, the government exists to support its residents so they can meet their basic needs.

122953.explanation: Public parking spaces should be used for parking regardless of the type of car. The municipality should invest extra in specific fast-charging points. There is enough space to realize these in places other than parking lots.

122955.explanation: Parking in your own neighborhood should be allowed without a permit; therefore, no more permit zones should be introduced.

122956.explanation: What we ask from residents, we should also ask from entrepreneurs and their suppliers. Very old vehicles with high emissions should no longer be allowed in city centers. Where support is needed during the transition, the municipality should help, for example by providing a depot.

122961.explanation: We increase these every year by an average of 2.5 cents, taking into account a fair balance between luxury hotels and our family campsites.

122962.explanation: Our current industrial areas have plenty of space, and good regional cooperation also brings balance without sacrificing residential or natural areas.

122964.explanation: The municipality must comply with the law, which currently prohibits this. Building more affordable homes quickly prevents status holders from unnecessarily staying in shelters.

122966.explanation: We face a major challenge in the energy transition, and where well integrated, we should install solar panels. Preferably fill all roofs and parking lots first, but if a good idea arises in the meantime, we should seize that opportunity as well.

122968.explanation: Maasniel, as well as all other neighborhoods, deserve a cultural center. Existing ones should be made more attractive, and where none exist, they should be realized as soon as possible in collaboration with the province.

122971.explanation: A coffeeshop should be allowed in Roermond where it causes no nuisance. Not like the old locations with all their problems.

122972.explanation: There are enough expensive homes in Roermond, so the focus should be on truly affordable housing. 100% affordable owner-occupied and rental homes that cost households no more than 25% of their net income-a very normal expectation according to SP.

122975.explanation: A place where people can come together, express culture and creativity, and where general services like a GP, physiotherapist, ATM, and others are available for a vibrant community.

122976.explanation: The duration has already been shortened and the price raised. The focus should be on making garages more attractive for motorists so people need to drive into the city center less to park. Then our residents also have shorter distances to walk home with their shopping.

122977.explanation: Not in the center, but one or two additional decent parking garages are welcome. That prevents driving into the center but keeps the area attractive.

122979.explanation: The focus should be on attracting more shoppers to the city center. Entrepreneurs have been promised this for years, but nothing has come of it.

122980.explanation: The homeless should be helped to get housing (and work), and if a shelter place is needed, it should preferably be near our care and safety services to keep an eye on things when help is needed.

122919.explanation: Leaders should be chosen based on quality and commitment, not on male/female gender. Everyone must have equal opportunities to participate, even if they do not fit into the male or female box.

122920.explanation: Residents must be able to co-decide on major decisions that affect their living environment.

122923.explanation: The municipality has the task to protect the livability, health, and peace of residents and animals. Complete freedom for hospitality can lead to structural nuisance, disruption of night rest, and deterioration of the living environment. Residents' and animals' peace takes precedence over commercial interests.

122925.explanation: New distribution centers lead to large box buildings in the landscape, extra traffic, emissions, and landscape damage, while providing little structural employment. We choose a local, sustainable economy instead of large-scale logistics.

122926.explanation: Refugees have the right to humane shelter. We stand for solidarity, human rights, and international responsibility. Problems in shelters require improvement, not closure.

122928.explanation: Wood burning causes severe air pollution, health problems, and CO₂ emissions. It is harmful to humans, animals, and the climate and does not fit with a sustainable and healthy living environment.

122930.explanation: Fireworks cause animal suffering, stress, air pollution, and health problems. Even a central show does not change that.

122931.explanation: We want less waste and more reuse. Unlimited free bulky waste collection can lead to faster disposal. At the same time, it must remain affordable. Therefore, we choose clear rules that stimulate reuse and prevent waste from ending up on streets or in nature.

122933.explanation: Accessibility is a right, not a favor. The municipality must support entrepreneurs in making buildings accessible for people with disabilities.

122935.explanation: Independent local journalism is important. This way, residents know what is happening and can participate in thinking along and decision-making.

122940.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on privacy and rarely prevents structural problems. Investing in social solutions works better.

122942.explanation: Safety is achieved by addressing problems in time and maintaining dialogue. Arming enforcement makes situations escalate faster. We want a safe public space without intimidating enforcement.

122948.explanation: Many MBO students live in Roermond who need affordable housing. At the same time, housing pressure also affects starters, families, and people with lower incomes. The municipality should primarily focus on structural, affordable, and sustainable housing solutions that work for everyone.

122949.explanation: We base ourselves on trust, not distrust. People on welfare often already live under great pressure. Mandatory counter-performances make people feel inferior and rarely lead to sustainable participation. Support and guidance are more effective.

122951.explanation: Due to higher housing and energy costs, people just above the income threshold do not receive help. We want to raise that threshold so that more people who need it receive support and can make ends meet.

122953.explanation: Electric driving reduces air pollution. Therefore, electric transport must be supported with sufficient charging stations. At the same time, electric cars do not solve everything. They take up a lot of space. Therefore, charging policy must be combined with more space for walking, cycling, and public transport.

122955.explanation: Parking permits can reduce parking nuisance and give residents more peace. They can also help limit the number of cars per household, creating more space for greenery and play.

122956.explanation: More car traffic causes more air pollution in the city. This is bad for residents' health and the livability of the city center. With an environmental zone, the municipality can exclude polluting vehicles from the center and thus ensure cleaner air.

122961.explanation: Tourism puts pressure on nature and the living environment. The proceeds from tourist tax must be used to limit the damage caused by tourism to nature and the environment, so residents do not bear these costs.

122962.explanation: New business parks cost space, greenery, and cause extra traffic. We first choose to improve and make existing business parks more sustainable, or where possible, transform them into housing or green areas.

122964.explanation: People with a residence permit have a right to housing. The housing shortage was not caused by people needing a home, but by too few affordable houses. By investing in this, we prevent exclusion and tensions.

122966.explanation: Solar energy is important, but agricultural land is scarce and urgently needed for food production and nature. Therefore, we first choose solar panels on roofs, parking lots, and along infrastructure.

122968.explanation: Roermond already has good cultural facilities, such as the Oranjerie and the ECI Culture Factory. Therefore, we currently see no clear necessity for another cultural center.

122971.explanation: A well-regulated coffeeshop provides clarity and prevents street dealing and nuisance. The current policy with an appropriate location works and must be carefully continued.

122972.explanation: Social rental housing is needed to keep Roermond affordable and livable. By making clear agreements about social rental during new construction, neighborhoods remain mixed and accessible for different incomes.

122975.explanation: Neighborhood centers are important places for meeting, support, and social cohesion. They help prevent loneliness and make neighborhoods stronger. We believe investing in neighborhood centers is investing in welfare, prevention, and connection between people.

122976.explanation: Less car traffic is good for air quality and the livability of the city center. More expensive paid parking can help reduce car use. It is important that measures are fair and take residents and people dependent on cars into account.

122977.explanation: More parking spaces take away space for greenery and play. Where a car stands, a child can no longer play, and there is no place for social interaction. We choose a center where people are central, not cars.

122979.explanation: Expansion of the Designer Outlet leads to more traffic and pressure on nature and the living environment. It also puts pressure on local and sustainable entrepreneurs. We choose local and sustainable entrepreneurs and quality over shopping tourism.

122980.explanation: People without a fixed residence deserve peace and support. A walk-in center offers help and prevents problems from worsening. That is humane and better for the city.

statements.title: Roermond should open a drop-in centre for homeless people near the station area.
Roermond’s residents should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Roermond moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Roermond.

titleParts.text: In Roermond moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Roermond should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the centre.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
There should be more camera surveillance in Roermond.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Roermond should introduce parking permits in more neighbourhoods.

titleParts.text: Roermond moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Roermond should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Roermond controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: At least 35% of newly built homes in Roermond should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Roermond moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: In Roermond, the business parks are allowed to expand.
Roermond must build a new cultural center in Maasniel.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Paid parking in the city center should become more expensive.
The plan to expand the Designer Outlet in Roermond must be scrapped.
